application.name: OctoBot
application.description: Terminal-native AI operations control center built with Rust, Ratatui, Tokio, and Axum.

features.name: Ratatui terminal interface
features.description: Keyboard-first terminal UI for operational monitoring and control.

features.name: Command console
features.description: Bottom command console with Tab autocomplete for supported operational commands.

features.name: Allowlisted command execution
features.description: Executes a small set of read-only infrastructure commands as real subprocesses.

features.name: Sandbox blocking
features.description: Unsupported, shell-wrapped, or write-oriented commands are blocked by the allowlist.

features.name: Streaming command output
features.description: Streams stdout and stderr into application state and the Logs view.

features.name: Command timeout
features.description: Infrastructure commands are terminated after 8 seconds.

features.name: Typed event bus
features.description: Uses OpsEvent with Tokio mpsc for runtime events.

features.name: Realtime state snapshots
features.description: Uses Tokio watch channels to publish OpsState snapshots to the UI and API.

features.name: State reducer
features.description: Central OpsState reducer applies events for commands, agents, workflows, reports, recovery, replay, plugins, runtimes, and graph data.

features.name: PostgreSQL persistence
features.description: Persists append-only events and current state tables through SQLx when configured.

features.name: Append-only event store
features.description: Every applied OpsEvent is serialized to PostgreSQL with its current OpsState snapshot when the database is configured.

features.name: Event replay engine
features.description: Loads persisted events in insertion order for replay.

features.name: Historical state reconstruction
features.description: Rebuilds OpsState by applying persisted events to an empty replay baseline.

features.name: Semantic memory retrieval
features.description: Embeds a query and searches Qdrant for operational memory.

features.name: Incident similarity search
features.description: Runs source-filtered semantic search over incident memory.

features.name: Operational memory indexing
features.description: Indexes incident, explainability, research, and command execution events into Qdrant when configured.

features.name: Incident detection command
features.description: Creates an incident event and starts the hardcoded incident workflow.

features.name: Incident table
features.description: Displays incident id, severity, service, status, and active hypothesis.

features.name: Incident replay
limitations: Replay is an in-memory cursor over retained events
Replay is not persisted as a full session player

features.name: Dynamic agent registry
features.description: Agents are registered from runtime events instead of startup fixtures.

features.name: Async agent runtime manager
features.description: Runtime manager handles agent registration and task lifecycle transitions inside the Tokio event loop.

features.name: Provider-backed AI agents
features.description: Provider-backed agents emit lifecycle, reasoning, telemetry, token usage, and persistent memory events.

features.name: Tool-calling request support
features.description: AI provider requests can include structured function/tool schemas and parse returned tool calls.

features.name: Spawn agent command
features.description: Registers a new research agent through the runtime event pipeline.

features.name: Coordination graph
features.description: Tracks agent-to-agent and workflow-to-agent links with protocol, message, confidence, and timestamp.

features.name: Docker API integration
features.description: Discovers containers through the Docker Engine HTTP API over a Unix socket.

features.name: Kubernetes API integration
features.description: Discovers pods through the Kubernetes API when configured or running in cluster.

features.name: Prometheus integration
features.description: Discovers live targets through the Prometheus up query.

features.name: Loki integration
features.description: Provides a real Loki query client when OCTOBOT_LOKI_URL is configured.

features.name: OpenSearch integration
features.description: Provides a real OpenSearch search client when OCTOBOT_OPENSEARCH_URL is configured.

features.name: Infrastructure discovery
features.description: Runtime emits InfrastructureSnapshotRecorded only when configured integrations return real nodes.

features.name: Topology mapping
features.description: Builds service, container, pod, database, cache, and vector-store relationship edges from discovered infrastructure nodes.

features.name: Dependency graph generation
features.description: Emits discovered topology edges into the knowledge graph and exposes a topology snapshot in state.

features.name: Research view
features.description: Shows research profile, evidence signals, knowledge graph links, and explainability records.

features.name: Research confidence refresh
features.description: Refreshes research confidence state from current signals.

features.name: Knowledge graph
features.description: Tracks nodes and edges for services, incidents, deployments, metrics, and recovery actions.

features.name: Research content
features.description: Research confidence is derived from explainability records, incidents, timeline events, knowledge edges, and agent memory signals.

features.name: Workflow monitor
features.description: Displays workflow id, name, owner, stage, and progress.

features.name: YAML DAG workflow definitions
features.description: Loads typed YAML workflow definitions with DAG validation, cycle detection, retry policy parsing, and approval checkpoint parsing.

features.name: Autonomous recovery queue
features.description: Tracks recovery proposals with target, status, required role, evidence, and risk.

features.name: Recovery proposal command
features.description: Creates a dry-run-only recovery action.

features.name: Recovery approval command
features.description: Approves or rejects recovery based on the current role.

features.name: Dry-run remediation gate
features.description: Write actions are recorded and approved in state, but destructive commands are dry-run only.

features.name: Explainability ledger
features.description: Records action, reason, evidence, confidence, tools used, and timestamp.

features.name: Reports view
features.description: Displays report queue entries and recent explainability records.

features.name: JSON report generation
features.description: Writes structured JSON reports containing state, incidents, workflows, executions, explainability, timeline, recovery actions, research profile, plugins, runtimes, knowledge graph, and sandbox policy.

features.name: Sandbox policy state
features.description: Updates role-aware sandbox approval state.

features.name: External integrations
features.description: Configured integrations are wired through provider clients, persistence, semantic memory, metrics, logs, search, and realtime task streams.

features.name: Infrastructure health table
features.description: Displays discovered infrastructure nodes and event-sampled health, CPU, and memory values.

features.name: Time-travel correlation timeline
features.description: Correlates deployments, incidents, commands, metrics, agent work, and recovery proposals.

knownLimitations: External services such as Ollama, PostgreSQL, Qdrant, Prometheus, Loki, OpenSearch, Docker, and Kubernetes require local configuration before their live integrations produce data.
Recovery actions remain dry-run only unless an operator explicitly extends the command policy.
The TUI replay cursor is in-memory, while persistent replay/reconstruction is available through the API.
